meta.name: BSQ-8C Body Shape Questionnaire Brief
meta.description: The BSQ-8C is an 8-item short form of the Body Shape Questionnaire (BSQ-34), assessing body shape concerns and dissatisfaction over the past four weeks. It is an effective screening tool for body image distress and eating disorder risk.
meta.author: Evans & Dolan (1993)
meta.reference: Evans C, Dolan B. Body Shape Questionnaire: derivation of shortened "alternate forms". Int J Eat Disord. 1993;13(3):315-321.
meta.instruction: The following questions ask about how you have been feeling about your appearance over the past four weeks. Please select the answer that best applies to you.

questions.text: Have you worried about your shape when bored?
Have you worried about your flesh not being firm enough?
Has seeing your reflection (e.g., in a mirror or shop window) made you feel bad about your shape?
Have you worried about parts of your body having too much fat?
Has being with other people made you notice your shape more?
Has looking at your shape in a mirror made you feel bad?
Have you felt dissatisfied with your figure?
Has thinking about your shape interfered with your ability to concentrate?

ranges.label: No Body Shape Concern
ranges.description: Your body shape concern is within the normal range with no significant body image distress.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy body image
Appreciate your body for its functionality rather than just appearance

ranges.label: Mild Body Shape Concern
ranges.description: You have mild body shape concerns and occasional dissatisfaction with your body.
suggestions: Be mindful of the impact of social media and culture on body image
Cultivate a positive attitude toward your body
Choose clothing and activities that make you feel comfortable

ranges.label: Moderate Body Shape Concern
ranges.description: You have moderate body shape concerns and dissatisfaction, which may affect daily life and self-esteem.
suggestions: Pay attention to mental health related to body image
Reduce frequent body checking with scales and mirrors
Consider discussing body image issues with a counselor
Practice mindfulness to cultivate body acceptance

ranges.label: Severe Body Shape Concern
ranges.description: You have severe body shape concerns and dissatisfaction, which may significantly affect quality of life and could be a risk factor for eating disorders.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional psychological help
Body image distress may be related to eating disorders; a comprehensive evaluation is recommended
Avoid extreme dieting or exercise behaviors
Seek supportive social environments and communities
